title: Phenol Chloroform DNA Extraction from Polycarbonate Filters
author: Unknown
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ibbcain

steps.title: If not using a whole filter,...
steps.instruction: If not using a whole filter, aseptically cut out a piece (e.g. a 1/2) of a filter. For aseptic technique, dip steel forceps and scissor into 95% ethanol, then flame forceps. Repeat twice.

steps.title: Put filter into sterile polypropylene tube
steps.instruction: Put filter into sterile polypropylene tube.

steps.title: Place tube on ice
steps.instruction: Place tube on ice.

steps.title: Add lysis buffer into tubes
steps.instruction: Add lysis buffer into tubes. See "Before you start" on options for a lysis buffer.

steps.title: Massage the filter with a flame-sterilized...
steps.instruction: Massage the filter with a flame-sterilized glass rod to suspend all the cells from the filter.

steps.title: Add lysozyme from a 100 mg/mL...
steps.instruction: Add lysozyme from a 100 mg/mL stock to a final concentration of 1 mg/mL.

steps.title: Incubate 20 minutes at 37ºC Ideally...
steps.instruction: Incubate 20 minutes at 37ºC Ideally on a shaker, but a water bath is ok.

steps.title: Add proteinase K (20 mg/ml stock solution)
steps.instruction: Add proteinase K (20 mg/ml stock solution). Final concentration of 50 μg/ml.

steps.title: Add 10% SDS, final concentration 0
steps.instruction: Add 10% SDS, final concentration 0.5%.

steps.title: Incubate 2 hours at 50ºC
steps.instruction: Incubate 2 hours at 50ºC. Ideally on a shaker, but a water bath is ok.

steps.title: Do all of the following in the fume hood
steps.instruction: Do all of the following in the fume hood.

steps.title: Add saturated phenol into tubes, 1/2...
steps.instruction: Add saturated phenol into tubes, 1/2 of the total aqueous volume. Saturated phenol can be purchased, rather than made, if preferred.

steps.title: Cap the tubes tightly, shake the contents well
steps.instruction: Cap the tubes tightly, shake the contents well. Allow it to sit for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Same as phenol, add equal volume...
steps.instruction: Same as phenol, add equal volume of chloroform isoamyl alcohol (2.5 mL) and repeat step 2. On a tabletop centrifuge, run for 1 minute on maximum speed.

steps.title: Transfer the aqueous (top) layter into sterile Oak Ridge-tubes
steps.instruction: Transfer the aqueous (top) layter into sterile Oak Ridge-tubes. Oak Ridge tubes.

steps.title: Add 2x the aqueous volume of...
steps.instruction: Add 2x the aqueous volume of ice cold absolute ethanol into tubes.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.1x the total volume of 10 M ammonium acetate.

steps.title: Mix the contents and place the...
steps.instruction: Mix the contents and place the tubes in -20ºC freezer for at least 1 hour, or overnight.

steps.title: Spin the tubes at 10,000 rpm for 35 minutes
steps.instruction: Spin the tubes at 10,000 rpm for 35 minutes.

steps.title: Carefully remove the supernatant
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the supernatant.

steps.title: Carefully wash the DNA pellet with...
steps.instruction: Carefully wash the DNA pellet with 1 mL of ice-cold 70% ethanol.

steps.title: Remove the 70% ethanol and place...
steps.instruction: Remove the 70% ethanol and place the tubes in sterile hood and let the DNA pellets dry for 2-3 hours, until completely dry.

steps.title: Resuspend the DNA into 200 μL of sterile TE buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend the DNA into 200 μL of sterile TE buffer. (pH 8)

steps.title: Measure the DNA concentration using the spectrophotometer and/or fluorometer
steps.instruction: Measure the DNA concentration using the spectrophotometer and/or fluorometer. https://www.protocols.io/view/nanodrop-spectrophotometer-nd-1000-for-nucleic-aci-id2ca8e

steps.title: Store DNA in a -20ºC freezer
steps.instruction: Store DNA in a -20ºC freezer.
